title: Cornercase. Verilog frontend exits with assert instead of an error message
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
Using `Yosys 0.9+3760 (git sha1 871fc34, clang 3.8.0-2ubuntu4 -fPIC -Os)`

    module top  # ( parameter AUTOPLACE = 0 )
       (
        input  rxck270,
        input  ck27start,
        output ubufclk135
        );
       
       generate
          if ( AUTOPLACE ) begin
             //             
          end else begin
             
             wire cmb_ck135 = ck27start ? 1'b1 : ~ubufclk135;           
             (* BEL="X1/Y11/lc3" *) SB_DFF reg_clk135(.Q(ubufclk135),.C(rxck270),.D(cmb_ck135));
    
             assign ubufclk135 = reg_clk135;   // This illegal statement trigger assert error.
             
          end
       endgenerate
    endmodule

## Expected behavior
An error message of some kind, on the illegal statement, for instance:

    top.v:20: ERROR: Failed to detect width for identifier \reg_clk135!

## Actual behavior

    ERROR: Assert `count_id(wire->name) == 0' failed in kernel/rtlil.cc:1801
